arts.ttl: 'Huge profit' - The transfer status of every Leicester City player - Jordan Blackwell - Leicestershire Live
arts.sum: Having signed a new three-year deal last summer, it doesn’t feel likely that Jannik Vestergaard is going anywhere any time soon. For Justin and Ricardo Pereira, while they will only have 12 months left on their contracts this summer, they would be expected to stay if City survive.

arts.ttl: Tottenham in early team news hint before Leicester City as Ange Postecoglou pressure mounts
arts.sum: The poor run of form has seen manager Postecoglou come under pressure, with Tottenham languishing in 15th place after 22 games. James Maddison has posted a pre-match graphic on his Instagram story, hinting that that he may start against Ruud van Nistelrooy's team.

arts.ttl: How to watch Spurs vs Leicester - TV channel, live stream, kick-off
arts.sum: How can I live stream Tottenham vs Leicester City and watch the Premier League game abroad? Tottenham Hotspur return to Premier League action this afternoon when they host Leicester City (2pm).

arts.ttl: Leicester star offered to Champions League club for January transfer
arts.sum: Leicester City defender Caleb Okoli has reportedly been offered to Bologna this January ahead of a likely potential exit. The 23-year-old hasn’t made much of an impression at Leicester, particularly since Ruud van Nistelrooy replaced Steve Cooper as Foxes manager. It now seems likely that Okoli could leave and return to Italy, though Van Nistelrooy […] The post Leicester star offered to Champions League club for January transfer appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: How to watch Tottenham vs Leicester City today with game not on TV
arts.sum: Spurs host Leicester on Sunday afternoon and the match will not be shown on UK TV due to broadcasting choices made by Sky Sports and TNT Sports

arts.ttl: The £165m Tottenham figure that will cause concern for Daniel Levy as announcement due
arts.sum: The report gave some early clues as to what the accounts will look like for Spurs for the 2023/24 financial year when they are released in the next few months, and also highlighted just how important it is for the club to ensure that they get themselves back into the conversation annually when it comes to a top four finish, something that seems very much beyond them this season, lying 15th and closer to the bottom three than the Champions League places. That fall was due to broadcast money falling because of a lack of Champions League football, with last year’s report taking into account the 2023/24 financial year when Spurs reached the last 16 of the competition and delivered €235m (£198.4m) in broadcast revenue.
